item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents strongly reject the statement from the opening through the final turn. They both acknowledge that social interests matter, but only as something constrained by individual rights; neither visibly shifts position or flips. Engagement is moderate-to-strong because each agent picks up the other's rights-as-boundary and majority/state-abuse framing, though the debate becomes largely reinforcing rather than adversarial.
